$comment: Single source of truth for the LMS product backlog. Consumed by scripts/github/seed-backlog.ps1 to create GitHub labels, milestones (epics) and issues (stories/tasks/bugs). Edit here, then re-run the seeder (idempotent by title).

milestones.title: M0 — Foundation
milestones.description: Monorepo, CI/CD, schema, tenancy primitives. (Largely scaffolded.)

milestones.title: M1 — Tenant + Identity MVP
milestones.description: Provision tenants/sub-tenants, SSO login, RBAC, rostering.

milestones.title: M5 — Scale & Commercialize
milestones.description: Billing, video, search, mobile, compliance hardening.

labels.name: type/epic
labels.description: A large body of work spanning many stories

labels.name: type/task
labels.description: Technical work, no direct user story

labels.name: area/integration
labels.description: School portal / LTI / SSO / embedding / sub-tenants

epics.title: EPIC: Multi-Tenancy, Sub-Tenants & Provisioning
epics.summary: A tenant is a customer (a district, university, or company). Many customers are hierarchical: a district buys the platform and each school under it needs its own admins, branding, SSO and reporting while rolling up to the district. This epic delivers the hybrid pool/silo model AND a first-class sub-tenant (parent->child) hierarchy so we can sell to districts and onboard their schools as sub-tenants.

stories.title: Provision a new tenant (pool tier)
stories.i_want: to create a tenant with a slug, name, region and plan
stories.so_that: a new customer can be onboarded in minutes onto shared infrastructure
ac: POST /tenants creates a control-plane row with tier=pool, status=provisioning->active
A unique subdomain (slug.lms.app) routes to the tenant
RLS context works immediately (no infra to stand up)
tenant.provisioned event emitted to the outbox

stories.title: Promote a tenant to silo (dedicated DB)
stories.i_want: to migrate a tenant to an isolated Neon database/branch
stories.so_that: enterprise customers get physical isolation, their own keys, or data residency
ac: Saga provisions a Neon project/branch and runs migrations
Tenant rows are bulk-copied; catalog database_ref repointed; tier flipped to silo
No application code change; schema identical pool<->silo
Rollback path if any saga step fails

stories.title: Create a district with school sub-tenants
stories.i_want: to register my schools as sub-tenants under the district
stories.so_that: each school is administered independently while the district sees consolidated data and billing
ac: Sub-tenant inherits plan/billing from parent unless overridden
District admin can list/search all child sub-tenants
Aggregate (cross-sub-tenant) reporting is scoped to the district subtree only
RLS still isolates each sub-tenant's row-level data

stories.title: Delegate sub-tenant administration
stories.i_want: to grant a school admin rights limited to their school
stories.so_that: schools self-manage without seeing sibling schools' data
ac: Role assignment can be scoped to a sub-tenant
School admin cannot read or write other sub-tenants

stories.title: Tenant resolution by subdomain, JWT and header
stories.i_want: every request to resolve its TenantContext deterministically
stories.so_that: pool/silo routing and RLS are always correct
ac: Resolution order: subdomain -> JWT tenantId -> X-Tenant-Id (service-to-service only)
Resolved context carries {tenantId, parentTenantId, tier, databaseUrl}

stories.title: Tenant offboarding & data export (FERPA/GDPR)
stories.i_want: to export or delete all of a school's data on contract end
stories.so_that: we honor data-ownership and right-to-erasure obligations
ac: GET /tenants/{id}/export returns OneRoster CSV + content archive
Deletion fans a purge across all services (verified)
Audit log records the export/delete with actor and hash chain

stories.title: Add tenant.parent_id + subtree helpers to schema & RLS
ac: Materialized path or recursive CTE helper for district subtree
RLS policies updated; rls.sql tenant_tables list kept in sync
schema.sql validated (pglast) and applied in CI

stories.title: White-label tenant branding
stories.i_want: to set my school's logo, colours, theme and custom domain
stories.so_that: the LMS looks like part of our own portal to students and staff
ac: tenant_branding stores logo, favicon, primary/secondary/accent colours, theme, custom_domain (unique), custom_css, support_email
Sub-tenants inherit unset fields from the parent (tenant_effective_branding); a district default can be overridden field-by-field
tenant_branding is under RLS (one tenant cannot read/write another's branding)

stories.title: Per-tenant governance settings
stories.i_want: to configure my own policy rules beyond roles (password rules, quiz lockdown, grading defaults, self-registration)
stories.so_that: each tenant enforces its own governance without affecting others
ac: tenant_setting is a per-tenant namespaced key -> JSON value store
PUT /tenants/{id}/settings/{key}; values validated against a known key catalog
Settings are read by the owning services to enforce behaviour

epics.title: EPIC: School Portal Integration & Embedding
epics.summary: Schools already have a portal/website. We must plug INTO it rather than replace it. This epic covers four integration surfaces: (1) LTI 1.3 / Advantage so the LMS launches from the school's existing portal/LMS; (2) SSO federation (SAML/OIDC) against the school's IdP; (3) white-label / embeddable widgets and theming per sub-tenant; (4) OneRoster/SIS sync so rosters flow from the school's SIS. Together these let a school drop our LMS into their site as if it were native.

stories.title: Launch the LMS from a school portal via LTI 1.3
stories.as_a: teacher using the school's existing portal
stories.i_want: to click a link in our portal and land in the LMS already logged in and in context
stories.so_that: staff and students never manage a second login
ac: LTI 1.3 OIDC login + resource link launch implemented
Roles mapped from LTI claims to LMS roles
Deep Linking 2.0 to embed specific courses/content
Names & Roles Provisioning Service (NRPS) roster pull

stories.title: Single sign-on against the school IdP (SAML/OIDC)
stories.i_want: to sign in with my school account
stories.so_that: I use one identity managed by my school
ac: JIT user provisioning on first login
Login fails closed if the IdP is misconfigured, with a clear admin error

stories.title: Per-sub-tenant white-label theming
stories.i_want: to apply our logo, colors and domain
stories.so_that: the LMS looks like part of our school's website
ac: Theme (logo, palette, favicon, custom domain) stored per sub-tenant
Web app resolves theme from TenantContext at the edge
Custom domain (school.lms.app or learn.school.edu) supported via Vercel domains
Falls back to district theme, then platform default

stories.title: Embeddable course/widget via iframe + signed token
stories.i_want: to embed a course card or dashboard widget in our CMS page
stories.so_that: students access learning without leaving the school site
ac: Signed, short-lived embed token scopes the widget to a tenant + resource
CSP/frame-ancestors restricted to the school's allowed origins

stories.title: Roster sync from the school SIS via OneRoster 1.2
stories.i_want: users, classes and enrollments to sync from our SIS
stories.so_that: we don't maintain rosters twice
ac: OneRoster 1.2 REST ingestion (orgs, users, classes, enrollments)
Idempotent upserts keyed on sourcedId via sis_id_map
Incremental delta sync on a schedule (QStash cron)
Conflict & error report surfaced to the admin

stories.title: Evaluate LTI Dynamic Registration for self-serve setup
ac: Determine if 1EdTech Dynamic Registration reduces per-school onboarding
Document tool/platform registration flow and risks

epics.title: EPIC: Identity, AuthN/AuthZ & RBAC
epics.summary: Authentication issues tenant-scoped tokens; authorization enforces granular, hierarchy-aware permissions. Roles can be scoped to an org unit (e.g., teacher of one section) and cascade down the org tree.

stories.title: Issue tenant-scoped access & refresh tokens
stories.i_want: to receive a JWT carrying my tenantId, tier, roles and entitlements
stories.so_that: downstream services authorize me correctly
ac: Claims include tenantId, parentTenantId, tier, scopes
Token verified at the gateway; rotation supported

stories.title: Granular, org-scoped RBAC
stories.i_want: to assign roles scoped to an org unit
stories.so_that: permissions follow the org hierarchy (district->school->course->section)
ac: role_assignment supports org_unit scope with cascade

stories.title: Manage roles & custom permission sets
stories.i_want: to create custom roles from permissions
stories.so_that: we match our institution's job functions
ac: System roles are read-only; custom roles editable

stories.title: Refresh token reuse must revoke the token family
ac: Revoke entire token family on reuse

stories.title: Provable isolation of per-tenant permission rules
stories.i_want: each tenant's roles and role->permission mappings to be isolated at the database engine, not just in code
stories.so_that: no tenant can ever read or alter another tenant's permission rules
ac: role, role_assignment and role_permission are all under RLS
role_permission (no tenant_id) is isolated via a join to its parent role's tenant_id
Automated CI test proves a cross-tenant read of role_permission returns zero rows
permission remains a shared, read-only capability vocabulary

epics.title: EPIC: Org Hierarchy & User/Roster Management
epics.summary: Within a (sub-)tenant, an org-unit tree models organization -> department -> semester -> course template -> course offering -> section/group. Users, roles and enrollments hang off this tree; this is the backbone the whole product queries against.

stories.title: Build and browse the org-unit tree
stories.i_want: to create departments, terms and sections under my school
stories.so_that: courses and people are organized the way we operate
ac: CRUD org_unit with typed levels and parent_id
Materialized path keeps subtree queries fast
Move/reparent updates the path of all descendants

stories.title: Invite and manage users
stories.i_want: to invite users by email and assign roles
stories.so_that: staff and students can access the right areas
ac: Bulk CSV import with validation report

stories.title: Guardian/parent relationships
stories.i_want: to be linked to my child's account
stories.so_that: I can view grades and announcements
ac: Guardian read-only access to scoped data

epics.title: EPIC: Courses & Curriculum
epics.summary: Authoring and delivering courses: templates that schools reuse term over term, offerings tied to a term/section, modules/units, and publish/lifecycle controls.

stories.title: Create a course from a reusable template
stories.i_want: to define a course template and spin up offerings each term
stories.so_that: we don't rebuild courses every semester
ac: Course template -> offering with copy of structure

stories.title: Organize course into modules/units with release conditions
stories.i_want: to sequence content into modules with availability dates
stories.so_that: students see material at the right time

stories.title: Course copy & cross-school sharing
stories.i_want: to push an approved course to multiple schools
stories.so_that: the district standardizes curriculum
ac: Copy a course to many sub-tenants/offerings

epics.title: EPIC: Content & Learning Materials (SCORM/xAPI)
epics.summary: Upload, store and deliver learning content (files, pages, SCORM packages) with completion tracking via xAPI, backed by Vercel Blob.

stories.title: Upload and serve content files
stories.i_want: to upload documents, slides and media
stories.so_that: students can access course materials
ac: Virus/type validation; size limits per plan

stories.title: Import and play SCORM packages with completion tracking
stories.i_want: to upload a SCORM 1.2/2004 package
stories.so_that: third-party courseware tracks completion and score

stories.title: Author rich pages in-platform
stories.i_want: a WYSIWYG editor for content pages
stories.so_that: I create materials without external tools

epics.title: EPIC: Enrollment & Registration
epics.summary: Place learners and instructors into course offerings/sections — manually, in bulk, via self-registration, or synced from SIS — with roles and dates.

stories.title: Enroll users into a section with a role
stories.i_want: to enroll students and teachers into sections
stories.so_that: they get access scoped to that class
ac: Bulk enroll from CSV or org-unit

stories.title: Student self-registration with approval
stories.i_want: to self-enroll into open courses
stories.so_that: I can pick electives

epics.title: EPIC: Assignments & Submissions
epics.summary: Create assignments, collect submissions (file/text/online), and route them to grading with due dates, late policies and feedback.

stories.title: Create an assignment and collect submissions
stories.i_want: to create an assignment with instructions, due date and submission type
stories.so_that: students submit work for grading

stories.title: Provide inline feedback and annotations
stories.i_want: to annotate and comment on a submission
stories.so_that: students get actionable feedback

stories.title: Group assignments
stories.i_want: to assign work to student groups
stories.so_that: teams submit once and share the grade

epics.title: EPIC: Assessments & Quizzing
epics.summary: Author question banks, build quizzes, deliver timed attempts with shuffling and pooling, and auto-grade objective items.

stories.title: Build a quiz from a question bank
stories.i_want: to create quizzes from reusable questions
stories.so_that: I assess learning efficiently and reuse items
ac: Question types: MCQ, multi-select, T/F, short answer, numeric, essay
Question bank with tagging and random pools

stories.title: Deliver a timed, secured attempt
stories.i_want: to take a quiz with a timer and saved progress
stories.so_that: my attempt is fair and resilient

stories.title: Auto-grade objective items and route essays
stories.i_want: objective questions graded instantly and essays queued
stories.so_that: I only hand-grade what needs judgment
ac: Essay items routed to manual grading

epics.title: EPIC: Grading & Gradebook
epics.summary: A gradebook with schemes, weighted categories, items from all activities, manual and auto grades, and final-grade calculation and release.

stories.title: Configure gradebook with weighted categories
stories.i_want: to define grade categories, weights and a grade scheme
stories.so_that: final grades compute the way my course requires

stories.title: Enter, override and release grades
stories.i_want: to grade items and release grades to students
stories.so_that: students see results when I'm ready

stories.title: Student grade view
stories.i_want: to see my grades and feedback
stories.so_that: I track my standing

epics.title: EPIC: Rubrics, Competencies & Outcomes
epics.summary: Standards-based education: rubrics for consistent grading, competency frameworks, and alignment of activities to learning outcomes with mastery tracking.

stories.title: Create and apply rubrics
stories.i_want: to grade with a criteria/levels rubric
stories.so_that: grading is consistent and transparent
ac: Rubric score maps to grade item

stories.title: Track competency mastery
stories.i_want: to align activities to competencies and track mastery
stories.so_that: we report standards-based progress

epics.title: EPIC: Discussions & Collaboration
epics.summary: Threaded discussions, moderation and participation grading to support social learning.

stories.title: Threaded course discussions
stories.i_want: to post and reply in course forums
stories.so_that: I learn collaboratively

stories.title: Moderation & graded participation
stories.i_want: to moderate posts and grade participation
stories.so_that: discussions stay healthy and count

epics.title: EPIC: Announcements & Notifications
epics.summary: Targeted announcements and multi-channel notifications (in-app, email, push) with per-user preferences.

stories.title: Post targeted announcements
stories.i_want: to announce to a course/section/role
stories.so_that: the right people are informed

stories.title: Notification preferences & digests
stories.i_want: to control which notifications I get and how
stories.so_that: I'm not overwhelmed

epics.title: EPIC: Calendar & Scheduling
epics.summary: A unified calendar aggregating due dates, events and sessions with external calendar feeds.

stories.title: Unified course & personal calendar
stories.i_want: to see all my due dates and events in one place
stories.so_that: I manage my time

stories.title: Define bell schedules and class periods
stories.i_want: to configure the daily bell schedule and named periods for my school
stories.so_that: classes, rooms and teachers can be slotted into a consistent timetable
ac: Create one or more bell schedules per school (org unit)
Each schedule has ordered periods with start/end times and a day pattern (e.g. A/B day, Mon-Fri)
Periods are tenant-isolated and reusable across sections

stories.title: Build the class timetable (section x period x room x teacher)
stories.i_want: to assign each section to periods, a room and an instructor within an academic session
stories.so_that: students and teachers have a clear recurring class schedule
ac: A timetable entry links a section (org unit) to a period, room, instructor and day(s)
Conflicts (same room/teacher/period overlap) are detected and flagged
Entries are scoped to an academic session and respect timezone

stories.title: See my personal timetable
stories.i_want: to view my recurring weekly timetable and have it merge into my calendar/iCal feed
stories.so_that: I always know where and when my classes are
ac: Personal timetable derived from enrollments/teaching assignments
Merged into the unified calendar and iCal subscription feed
Reflects room, period times and day pattern

epics.title: EPIC: Analytics & Reporting (Caliper/LRS)
epics.summary: Capture Caliper/xAPI events into an LRS, build read-model summaries, and expose dashboards and exports for teachers, admins and districts (including cross-sub-tenant roll-ups).

stories.title: Capture learning events (Caliper/xAPI)
stories.i_want: to record standardized learning events
stories.so_that: analytics and interoperability are possible
ac: Caliper event ingestion to LRS tables

stories.title: Teacher engagement dashboard
stories.i_want: to see who's at risk and engagement trends
stories.so_that: I intervene early

stories.title: District roll-up reporting across schools
stories.i_want: to compare schools within my district
stories.so_that: I allocate support
ac: Roll-up scoped to district subtree only
Respects sub-tenant isolation for row data

epics.title: EPIC: AI & Personalization (Groq)
epics.summary: LLM-powered help (Groq) grounded in course content via pgvector RAG: tutoring, question generation, feedback assist, and content search.

stories.title: AI study assistant grounded in course content
stories.i_want: to ask questions and get answers from my course materials
stories.so_that: I get help 24/7 without leaving the LMS

stories.title: AI-assisted quiz question generation
stories.i_want: to generate draft questions from a topic/reading
stories.so_that: I build assessments faster

epics.title: EPIC: Video & Media
epics.summary: Upload, transcode (FFmpeg workers), caption and stream video with view analytics.

stories.title: Upload and stream lecture video
stories.i_want: to upload video that students can stream
stories.so_that: I deliver recorded lessons

epics.title: EPIC: Search
epics.summary: Fast, tenant-scoped search across courses, content, people and discussions (Postgres FTS/trgm + pgvector semantic).

stories.title: Global tenant-scoped search
stories.i_want: to search everything I can access
stories.so_that: I find content and people quickly

epics.title: EPIC: Billing & Subscriptions
epics.summary: Plans, subscriptions, usage metering (per active user/FTE) and invoicing, with district-level consolidated billing across sub-tenants.

stories.title: Subscribe a tenant to a plan
stories.i_want: to put a customer on a plan with seats/add-ons
stories.so_that: we charge correctly

stories.title: Usage metering & invoicing
stories.i_want: to meter active users and generate invoices
stories.so_that: usage-based plans bill accurately

epics.title: EPIC: Security, Audit & Compliance
epics.summary: Cross-cutting security: hash-chained audit log, RLS verification, secrets hygiene, rate limiting, and FERPA/GDPR/COPPA controls.

stories.title: Tamper-evident audit log
stories.i_want: an immutable, verifiable record of sensitive actions
stories.so_that: we can prove what happened

stories.title: Automated RLS isolation tests in CI
ac: Seed two tenants; assert no cross-tenant reads/writes
Run against the Postgres service in CI
Fail the build on any leak

stories.title: Per-tenant rate limiting at the gateway
stories.i_want: to rate-limit by tenant
stories.so_that: one tenant can't degrade others

stories.title: COPPA/age-appropriate handling for K-12
stories.i_want: age-gated data handling and parental consent
stories.so_that: we comply with K-12 regulations

epics.title: EPIC: Mobile (BFF + apps)
epics.summary: A mobile backend-for-frontend and apps delivering core learner flows with offline-friendly patterns and push notifications.

stories.title: Mobile learner core flows via BFF
stories.i_want: to view courses, submit work and get notifications
stories.so_that: I learn on the go
ac: Auth via the same token model

epics.title: EPIC: Platform, CI/CD & Observability
epics.summary: Developer experience and operability: monorepo tooling, pipelines, environments, tracing/logging/metrics, and runbooks.

stories.title: Per-service deploy pipeline to GHCR + container host
ac: paths-filter matrix builds only changed services
Images pushed to GHCR with SHA + semver tags

stories.title: Local dev: docker-compose Postgres + Upstash emulation
ac: One command brings up DB + cache

stories.title: Choose container host (Fly vs Render vs Railway)
ac: Compare cost, cold start, regions, scaling

stories.title: AI-agent contribution ruleset (AGENTS.md)
stories.i_want: a single ruleset that every AI agent must read and follow before touching the repo
stories.so_that: all AI contributions are consistent, safe and traceable
ac: AGENTS.md at repo root defines mandatory rules (user-story-first, RLS for new tenant tables, pglast validation, ASCII-only PS scripts, idempotent seeder, no Copilot co-author trailer)
Every new feature is captured as a user story in backlog.json (and a GitHub issue) before/with implementation
Each change links the issue it implements
The ruleset is referenced from README and CONTRIBUTING

stories.title: Multi-agent delegation protocol
stories.i_want: a defined set of agent roles that hand work off to each other and never silently drop a task
stories.so_that: complex work is decomposed and delegated reliably with no dead ends
ac: AGENTS.md defines roles (orchestrator, backlog, schema, service, review) and a hand-off protocol
Every task is either completed or explicitly delegated to a named role - no agent denies/drops it
Hand-offs are logged (issue comment / commit trailer referencing the role)
An escalation path exists when no role can accept

epics.title: EPIC: Accessibility & Internationalization
epics.summary: WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility and multi-language support so the platform is usable by all learners and sellable globally.

stories.title: WCAG 2.2 AA across core flows
stories.i_want: fully keyboard- and screen-reader-accessible pages
stories.so_that: I can learn independently

stories.title: Internationalization & localization
stories.i_want: the UI in my language
stories.so_that: I can use the platform comfortably

epics.title: EPIC: Attendance & Participation
epics.summary: Take, track and report class attendance with per-tenant attendance codes, timetable-driven rosters and exports for compliance/SIS.

stories.title: Configure per-tenant attendance codes
stories.i_want: to define my own attendance codes and how each maps to present/absent/tardy/excused
stories.so_that: attendance reflects our school's policy and reporting categories
ac: Each tenant owns its set of attendance codes (label + category + flags)
Sensible defaults seeded; codes are tenant-isolated via RLS
Codes drive the options teachers see when taking attendance

stories.title: Take attendance for a class meeting
stories.i_want: to open a class meeting and mark each student present/absent/tardy/excused
stories.so_that: attendance is recorded accurately for every session
ac: Roster is derived from the section enrollment (and timetable entry when present)
One record per student per meeting; teacher can edit until the session is finalized
Marking emits an event so notifications/analytics can react

stories.title: View attendance summaries and flags
stories.i_want: to see attendance rates and chronic-absence flags by student, section and date range
stories.so_that: I can intervene early and meet reporting duties
ac: Configurable chronic-absence threshold raises a flag
Exportable (CSV/OneRoster) for compliance and SIS sync

stories.title: Parents and students see attendance
stories.i_want: to see attendance history and be notified of absences/tardies
stories.so_that: we stay informed and can resolve errors quickly
ac: Attendance history visible in the learner/guardian view
Only the guardian's linked children are visible (tenant + relationship scoped)
